decks.name: Artmage

methodology.summary: Same shell as Power Patron — Medius the Pure + Artmage Power Patron Fusion line into Artmage Fusion monsters/traps for negation, closing on Nerva + the Nervedo negate. (The YCS Columbus Top 8 deck was 'Power Patron Artmage'.)
methodology.endboard: Nerva the Power Patron of Creation, Medius the Pure, Artmage Power Patron
methodology.howItWins: Same shell as Power Patron — Medius the Pure + Artmage Power Patron Fusion line into Artmage Fusion monsters/traps for negation. Pure-Artmage as its own top deck is largely unverified; treat as the Power Patron Artmage hybrid.
methodology.weaknesses: Reactive Fusion ladder — Ash/Droll on Medius collapses it; clear the Field Spell to strip Nerva's destruction immunity; the Artmage trap that bounces all your S/T (with 3+ monster Types) means don't over-rely on backrow.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Branded / Despia

methodology.summary: Aluber → search Branded Fusion → Branded Fusion (Albaz + a DARK/LIGHT fodder from hand/deck) into Mirrorjade, or Albion the Sanctifire to chain more Branded spells; set Branded Banishment for an opponent-turn fusion + recovery.
methodology.endboard: Mirrorjade the Iceblade Dragon, Masquerade the Blazing Dragon, Branded in Red, Dramaturge of Despia
methodology.howItWins: Albaz Fusion midrange — Branded Fusion / Branded in Red/White summon a wall of Fusion bosses (often on YOUR turn), then grind with recursion + burn. Control/midrange, not an OTK.
methodology.weaknesses: Midrange, not explosive — loses the race to faster combo (Kewl Tune / Artmage) if it can't land Mirrorjade + backrow. Nibiru mid-combo (5+ summons), Droll before Branded Fusion, and non-targeting outs (Droplet / Evenly) all hurt.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Clown Crew

methodology.summary: Special Summon Clown Crew monsters from Deck/Extra Deck ignoring conditions (they are EFFECT-LOCKED until the end of the next turn) → use them as TRIBUTE for Clown Crew Biancaviso + a Vanity-style Special-Summon floodgate. Grinds behind the SS-lock.
methodology.endboard: Vanity's Fiend, Vanity's Ruler, Clown Crew Biancaviso
methodology.howItWins: Tribute-Summon FLOODGATE control — makes disposable tribute fodder, Tribute Summons Clown Crew Biancaviso (draw / negate face-up cards) AND Tribute-Summon-only floodgates (Vanity's Ruler / Vanity's Fiend) to lock you out, then grinds. Splashes a Nouvelles ritual package. Rogue, not tier-1.
methodology.weaknesses: Low turn-1 disruption beyond the floodgate (the summoned fuel is effect-locked) — break the lock and the board is fairly inert. Evenly Matched is a near-clean answer. Early Ash/Imperm on the engine stops the plan before the lock exists.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Dracotail (Branded)

methodology.summary: Normal Lukias → add Faimena → discard Faimena (Quick — works on EITHER turn) to Fusion Summon; Lukias/Pan as material SET Dracotail traps (Pan also destroys) → Arthalion (bounces, self-revives) + Branded Fusion for Mirrorjade. Gulamel = a free pop on EVERY Dracotail activation.
methodology.endboard: Dracotail Mululu, Dracotail Shaulas, Dracotail Arthalion, Mirrorjade the Iceblade Dragon
methodology.howItWins: Fusion combo/control — Dracotail Faimena is a near-one-card starter (discard to Fusion Summon), chaining Dracotail fusions + a trap suite; often splashes the Branded engine for Mirrorjade.
methodology.weaknesses: Banishment beats it (engine self-revives from GY); Nibiru mid-loop; a precise early Imperm on Lukias starves it; Droll before the search chain. Open Nibiru + Droplet/Evenly going second is its nightmare. (This is the YCS Columbus 2026 WINNING deck — respect it.)

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Elfnote

methodology.summary: Elfnote 'Singer' fairies (Lucina / Tinia / Regina / Fortuna) + the Power Patron engine (Elfnote Power Patron, Medius the Pure, Terminus portal) Synchro-climb through the Resonator / Red-Dragon-Archfiend package into RDA / Crimson King / Crystal Wing + Junoldo, recurring with Welcome Home / Rhapsodia traps.
methodology.endboard: Red Dragon Archfiend, Crystal Wing Synchro Dragon, Elfnote Seraphim Strelitzia, Elfnotes: Rhapsodia of Madness
methodology.howItWins: 'Singer' fairies shuffle into the center zone to trigger + Synchro-climb. Elfnote Power Patron raises a center monster's Level +3 and immediately Synchro Summons — laddering into a wall of Synchro negates capped by Junora's WIDE field-negate.
methodology.weaknesses: Over-extends + searches a LOT — Maxx C / Droll / Fuwalos / Purulia wreck it (the list mains 3 Fuwalos + 2 Droll ITSELF). Bystials hit its DARK/LIGHT Synchro GY. Nibiru on the climb.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Fairy Tail Magistus

methodology.summary: Fairy Tail (Lv4 Spellcasters + the unbanned Snow; Wiccat 'double Foolish' dumps Snow) feeds Magistus: equip Artemis → add Crowley → SS Crowley → Xyz Rilliona → SS Zoroa of Flame → Synchro into Zoroa of Daimon (Extra-Deck-effect floodgate), recurring Crowley/Rilliona to re-equip Magistus.
methodology.endboard: Zoroa, the Magistus of Daimon, Teller of the Magistus, Zoroa, the Magistus Verethragna, Fairy Tail - Snow
methodology.howItWins: Spellcaster engine fusing the Magistus equip package with Fairy Tail control. Fairy Tail - Wiccat 'double Foolish' fuels a search/recursion loop; ends on negates and grinds with Snow recursion + Magistus equips.
methodology.weaknesses: Combo-fragile — one Imperm/Ash at the Crowley/Rilliona pivot leaves only Fairy Tail bodies. Soft going first if it draws too many Fairy Tail bricks. Lightning Storm / Evenly / Cosmic cleanly answer the Zoroa board. Modest raw damage.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Kewl Tune

methodology.summary: Open a Kewl Tune Tuner + Synchro Overtake / Kewl Tune Synchro → Synchro-climb through the Kewl Tune Extra-Deck line (Track Maker / RS / Remix / B2B) into a capping board, ending on Super Starslayer TY-PHON (Special-Summon lock) plus Synchro disruption. Fydraulis Harmonia and JJ "Kewl Tune" extend / recover.
methodology.endboard: Super Starslayer TY-PHON - Sky Crisis, Kewl Tune Track Maker, Fydraulis Harmonia
methodology.howItWins: Music-themed Synchro Tuner combo — the Kewl Tune monsters + Synchro Overtake / Kewl Tune Synchro climb the Synchro ladder fast and consistently, ending on a capping board (Super Starslayer TY-PHON locks Special Summons) plus Synchro disruption. A proactive going-first combo deck that also mains its own handtrap suite (Veiler / Ash / Belle / Fuwalos).
methodology.weaknesses: Combo-reliant — Maxx C / Droll / Nibiru and non-targeting going-second sweepers (Droplet / Evenly) all bite; it leans hard on its Synchro Extra Deck, so Extra-Deck hate + non-destruction removal get under the board. (Newer archetype — this is an initial intel pass; refine as results settle.)

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Lunalight

methodology.summary: Black Sheep (discard → add a Lunalight S/T; banish from GY = Fusion substitute) + Polymerization → Perfume Dancer (search Tiger, recur) → Kaleido Chick sends material, Tiger revives → climb the Dance-Fusion ladder to Liger Dancer.
methodology.endboard: Lunalight Liger Dancer, Lunalight Silver Hound, S:P Little Knight
methodology.howItWins: Going-first Fusion combo (post-Duelist Advance) — a one-card Gold Leo line builds a sticky Liger Dancer board, then double-attacks + grinds via GY recursion.
methodology.weaknesses: Far better going SECOND than first — turn-1 interaction outside Liger / S:P is thin. Needs Polymerization live (Solemn / Poly-hate stalls it). Crushed by attribute/type hate (Shadow-Imprisoning Mirror, Archnemeses Protos, Bystials). ROGUE / locals-strong.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Maliss

methodology.summary:
Maliss

White Rabbit (Normal/SS → SET a Maliss Trap from Deck) → activate that trap THE TURN it's set by banishing a face-up Maliss (which self-revives — free bodies): TB-11 SS from Deck, GWC-06 search + banish, MTP-07 revive → climb to the Queens (Hearts Crypter). The @Ignister package turns leftover bodies into Accesscode / extra Links.
methodology.endboard: Maliss Q Hearts Crypter, Maliss in the Mirror, Maliss <C> TB-11, Maliss <C> MTP-07
methodology.howItWins: Cyberse Link-climbing combo that abuses BANISHMENT — Special Summons monsters back from banish to chain into Link bosses + a self-recurring loop, then grinds with the Maliss <C> disruption traps. Can OTK with Accesscode.
methodology.weaknesses: Post-banlist consistency is shakier; DROLL completely walls it (it adds repeatedly); the turn-1 board is low-impact without @Ignister; folds to spot removal of its on-board Maliss (bricks the <C> traps).

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Mitsurugi-Ryzeal

methodology.summary: Ryzeal starter → Rank-4 / King of the Feral Imps (the BRIDGE) → fetch the Mitsurugi enabler → run the ritual chain (Habakiri → Murakumo) AND bank Ryzeal Detonator (free repeatable destroy). Ends ~ Murakumo + Detonator + a Caesar/Photon negate + traps.
methodology.endboard: Ame no Murakumo no Mitsurugi, Ryzeal Detonator, D/D/D Wave High King Caesar, Mitsurugi Prayers
methodology.howItWins: Mitsurugi grind core + a Rank-4 Ryzeal package (Ryzeal Detonator = free repeatable destroy). More going-second power and more layers than pure Mitsurugi.
methodology.weaknesses: Combos through a NARROW bridge (King of the Feral Imps / the enabler) — handtrap that point and it folds. Droll brutal (two search engines). Extra-Deck-reliant (Droplet / ED hate bites).

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Mitsurugi

methodology.summary: Searcher (Saji / Aramasa — self-tributing floaters) → add Ame no Habakiri + Mitsurugi Prayers / Ritual → Ritual-Summon up to Ame no Murakumo (on Special Summon: Raigeki-style destroy, once/turn). Tributing Mitsurugi monsters triggers their GY floats, so it reloads as it combos.
methodology.endboard: Ame no Murakumo no Mitsurugi, Futsu no Mitama no Mitsurugi, Mitsurugi Great Purification, Mitsurugi Prayers
methodology.howItWins: Reptile Ritual engine — one starter (Ame no Habakiri) becomes a wall of recurring Ritual monsters + searched traps, then grinds behind repeated negates and a board-wipe.
methodology.weaknesses: Search-dependent — Droll + double handtraps choke it; lower disruption density than top combo; Special-Summon / Ritual-lock floodgates gut it; non-destruction removal walks through its protection. (YCS Columbus 2026 Top 8.)

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Power Patron

methodology.summary: Medius the Pure adds/SS Artmage Power Patron → Fusion-summon up the Power Patron ladder (Vidolium / Nervedo) → Nerva the Power Patron of Creation (destruction-immune while a card is active in the Field Spell Zone) + the Nervedo Pendulum negate; Artmage Fusion/traps add negation. (YCS Columbus 2026 Top 8.)
methodology.endboard: Nerva the Power Patron of Creation, Medius the Pure, Artmage Power Patron
methodology.howItWins: Pendulum-flavored Fusion ladder — Medius the Pure adds/Special Summons Artmage Power Patron, Fusion-summons up the line, closes on Nerva (destruction-immune while a Field Spell is active) + the Nervedo Pendulum negate. Usually the Power Patron Artmage hybrid.
methodology.weaknesses: Reactive — its negate triggers off ITS plays, so on YOUR turn you mostly fear held handtraps. Ash / Droll on Medius collapses it. Clearing the Field Spell strips Nerva's immunity.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Predaplant

methodology.summary: Predaprimitive (send a Predap to GY + tutor Polymerization; locks you to Plant/Dragon) or Verte Anaconda → spread Predator Counters (drop foes to Lv1) → Dragostapelia (negates effects of monsters carrying a counter) + Starving Venom Wing Dragon (sprays more counters) → climb to Predaplant Triphyovertum (negates Extra-Deck SS, gains ATK per counter, self-revives).
methodology.endboard: Predaplant Dragostapelia, Mirrorjade the Iceblade Dragon, Branded Fusion
methodology.howItWins: Albaz/Branded fusion toolbox — spreads Predator Counters (turning your monsters into Lv1 fusion fodder), negates with Dragostapelia, OTKs with Triphyovertum. Mostly a Genesys/OCG/rogue pick in TCG.
methodology.weaknesses: Reactive setup deck — one Ash/Imperm on Predaprimitive/Verte collapses the turn; slower than top combo; GY-banish hate stops Triphyovertum recursion. ROGUE in TCG (best evidence is an OCG regional Top 8).

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Radiant Typhoon

methodology.summary: Radiant Typhoon + Sky Striker HYBRID (YCS Columbus Top 4) — quick-play Spells (Super Poly, Droplet, MST) trigger Radiant Typhoon monsters; the Sky Striker engine (Engage) draws + grinds. Ends on a low board with set Spells + a monster-negate boss; Mandate turns MST into an omni-negate.
methodology.endboard: Varuroon, Radiant Typhoon Mandate, Sky Striker Ace - Shizuku, Sky Striker Mecha - Widow Anchor
methodology.howItWins: Quick-Play-Spell control/grind — its monsters trigger off activating quick-plays (Super Poly, Forbidden Droplet, Called by). Grinds with searchers + a monster-negate boss, recycles quick-plays, wins the long game. Often a Runick hybrid.
methodology.weaknesses: Control/grind — loses to multi-negate boards and to Anti-Spell Fragrance (Spell-heavy). It folds if its searchers (Krosea / Engage) are denied.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Sky Striker

methodology.summary: It's a SETUP turn, not a combo: dump 3+ Spells to GY (Engage etc.), keep your OWN monster zones EMPTY (Spells get bonuses when you control no monsters), end on Shizuku + 4–5 set Spells (Widow Anchor / Shark Cannon / Eagle Booster) + Multirole. Raye flips to Kagari (recur Engage) / Shizuku (search) reactively.
methodology.endboard: Sky Striker Ace - Shizuku, Sky Striker Mecha Modules - Multirole, Sky Striker Mecha - Widow Anchor, Sky Striker Mecha - Shark Cannon
methodology.howItWins: Spell-based control/grind — loops Sky Striker Mobilize - Engage! via the Ace monsters to out-resource you, disrupts with Widow Anchor / Shark Cannon. Wants its OWN monster zones EMPTY (most Quick-Plays need controlling no monsters).
methodology.weaknesses: Grind deck with a LOW ceiling — loses to multi-negate boards it can't out one Spell at a time, and to Anti-Spell Fragrance / Imperial Order (its whole deck is Spells). Heavy backrow removal + going-second OTKs that ignore its low board hurt.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Vanquish Soul

methodology.summary: VS monsters (all EARTH / FIRE / DARK) reveal monsters in hand for attribute effects; Razen searches; Stake your Soul! / VS Start! tutor. Heavy Borger / Caesar Valius SS themselves by returning a VS of a different Type. The K9 half bolts on a reactive Rank-5 Xyz toolbox (Ripper / Izuna / Lupus) that punishes the opponent's monster-effect activations.
methodology.endboard: Vanquish Soul Caesar Valius, Vanquish Soul Pluton HG, K9-17 Ripper
methodology.howItWins: Vanquish Soul K9 — a hand-resource 'tag-fighter' beatdown: reveal monsters in hand to fuel attribute effects, tag fighters in/out, grind resources. K9 adds bodies + punishes handtraps.
methodology.weaknesses: Grind/control with a modest turn-1 board — combo decks that go UNDER it deny K9 its triggers; it must HOLD reveal fodder; Spell/Trap interaction (Imperm / Evenly / Droplet) avoids feeding K9; Dimension Shifter hurts K9's GY summons. Strong-rogue post-banlist, not Tier-1.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: White Forest (Azamina)

methodology.summary: White Forest spells (Silvy / Elzette / Rciela) trigger off Synchro Summons + Sinful Spoils to grind cards, Synchro-climbing (Diabellstar / Azamina) into negates + non-targeting banish; the Azamina splash adds Ilia Silvia (a recyclable omni-negate).
methodology.endboard: Azamina Ilia Silvia, Chaos Angel, S:P Little Knight, Sinful Spoils of the White Forest
methodology.howItWins: Synchro Spellcasters/Illusions whose Special Summons + Sinful Spoils cards trigger White Forest spells to grind, then lock you out with a stacked multi-disruption board. Azamina splash adds a recyclable negate (Ilia Silvia).
methodology.weaknesses: Turns your DEAD handtraps into fuel, so ONE rarely ends them — but Droll chokes the search loop, non-targeting/non-destruction is needed, and Bystials hit their GY.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

decks.name: Yummy (Azamina)

methodology.summary: Yummy core (Marshmao SS's itself if you control no / only-LIGHT-Beast; Cooky/Cupsy swarm + dig) → pivot to Fiendsmith: Tract (search a LIGHT Fiend + discard; GY-banish to Fusion) → Requiem → tribute to SS Engraver from Deck, equip → Necroquip Princess → Sequence shuffles Lacrima + 2 Engraver for Desirae (negate). Azamina adds Ilia Silvia.
methodology.endboard: Azamina Ilia Silvia, Fiendsmith's Desirae, S:P Little Knight, Yummy Snatchy
methodology.howItWins: LIGHT-Beast Synchro combo (Cupsy/Cooky/Lollipo + Yummy Way Synchros) that swarms cheaply and, in the Azamina/Fiendsmith build, converts into multiple Fusion omni-negates.
methodology.weaknesses: GY-DEPENDENT — Dimension Shifter / macro is brutal; handtrap-vulnerable at the Fiendsmith pivot (Ash on Tract / Imperm on Engraver stops the disruption suite); Droll mid-combo; Nibiru on the multi-summon turn.

decks.notes: Imported from a ygoprodeck tournament list (May 2026). Refine as the meta shifts.

formats.name: Meta - May 2026

matchups.howTheyWin: Same shell as Power Patron — Medius the Pure + Artmage Power Patron Fusion line into Artmage Fusion monsters/traps for negation. Pure-Artmage as its own top deck is largely unverified; treat as the Power Patron Artmage hybrid.
matchups.gameplanFirst: Their negate triggers off responding to their OWN effects — play around held handtraps rather than a board negate. Bait the Artmage negate trap with a non-essential effect, then resolve your payoff.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Use non-destruction removal (boss leans on Field-Spell destruction immunity) — banish/bounce/tribute. BEWARE the Artmage trap that bounces ALL your Spells/Traps to hand if they control 3+ monster Types — don't over-rely on backrow. Clear the Field Spell, then remove the boss.
matchups.chokepointTheirs: Ash/Droll Medius the Pure (the search that assembles the Fusion). Veiler/Imperm the first Artmage or Power Patron effect to stop the Fusion + negate sequence.
matchups.comboLine: Same shell as Power Patron — Medius the Pure + Artmage Power Patron Fusion line into Artmage Fusion monsters/traps for negation, closing on Nerva + the Nervedo negate. (The YCS Columbus Top 8 deck was 'Power Patron Artmage'.)
matchups.weaknesses: Reactive Fusion ladder — Ash/Droll on Medius collapses it; clear the Field Spell to strip Nerva's destruction immunity; the Artmage trap that bounces all your S/T (with 3+ monster Types) means don't over-rely on backrow.
targetEndboard: Nerva the Power Patron of Creation

matchups.howTheyWin: Albaz Fusion midrange — Branded Fusion / Branded in Red/White summon a wall of Fusion bosses (often on YOUR turn), then grind with recursion + burn. Control/midrange, not an OTK.
matchups.gameplanFirst: They run few hard negates, so a clean board usually beats them — but DON'T over-commit LIGHT/DARK monsters into an open Super Polymerization, and respect Bystials (Druiswurm / Lubellion banishing your LIGHT/DARK).
matchups.gameplanSecond: Mirrorjade is the threat and it PUNISHES removal — if it leaves the field by YOUR card it wipes your monsters at the End Phase. Out it with NON-destruction: your own Super Polymerization (eats it as fusion material) is the best tech, or banish/bounce, or spend its banish first then push. Clear Branded backrow.

counterCards.name: Ash Blossom & Joyous Spring
counterCards.notes: On the REAL Branded Fusion, not the bait

counterCards.name: Nibiru, the Primal Being
counterCards.notes: They extend with many summons, few negates

counterCards.name: Super Polymerization
counterCards.notes: Eats Mirrorjade/Chimera — non-targeting, dodges End-Phase wipe

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Branded Fusion is the dream Ash — but good players BAIT it with Aluber / Frightfur Patchwork / Branded Opening first, so hold Ash for the actual Branded Fusion resolution (the fuse-from-deck). Second target: Imperm on Albion's fusion-extender mid-combo. Droll only lands if BEFORE Branded Fusion is searched.
matchups.comboLine: Aluber → search Branded Fusion → Branded Fusion (Albaz + a DARK/LIGHT fodder from hand/deck) into Mirrorjade, or Albion the Sanctifire to chain more Branded spells; set Branded Banishment for an opponent-turn fusion + recovery.
matchups.weaknesses: Midrange, not explosive — loses the race to faster combo (Kewl Tune / Artmage) if it can't land Mirrorjade + backrow. Nibiru mid-combo (5+ summons), Droll before Branded Fusion, and non-targeting outs (Droplet / Evenly) all hurt.

matchups.howTheyWin: Tribute-Summon FLOODGATE control — makes disposable tribute fodder, Tribute Summons Clown Crew Biancaviso (draw / negate face-up cards) AND Tribute-Summon-only floodgates (Vanity's Ruler / Vanity's Fiend) to lock you out, then grinds. Splashes a Nouvelles ritual package. Rogue, not tier-1.
matchups.gameplanFirst: Resolve your board BEFORE they Tribute Summon a Vanity floodgate, and end on disruption that does NOT need to Special Summon (set traps / in-hand handtraps) so a resolved Vanity's Ruler/Fiend doesn't brick your follow-up.
matchups.gameplanSecond: The lock is a Special-Summon floodgate — DON'T fight it with Special Summons. EVENLY MATCHED is the premier out (non-SS, non-targeting, banishes their board face-down to 1). DRNM / Droplet turn off the body. ⚠ Kaiju / Lava Golem REQUIRE a Special Summon — DEAD if the lock is 'neither player' (verify Biancaviso's wording). Clear the floodgate first, then commit SS.

counterCards.name: Dark Ruler No More
counterCards.notes: Turns off Vanity's lock for the turn — no SS needed

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Ash/Imperm the engine card that GENERATES the tribute fuel — NOT the locked fuel monsters (their effects are off). Handtrap BEFORE the floodgate resolves; once 'cannot Special Summon' is live, YOUR Special-Summon handtraps are dead too.
matchups.comboLine: Special Summon Clown Crew monsters from Deck/Extra Deck ignoring conditions (they are EFFECT-LOCKED until the end of the next turn) → use them as TRIBUTE for Clown Crew Biancaviso + a Vanity-style Special-Summon floodgate. Grinds behind the SS-lock.
matchups.weaknesses: Low turn-1 disruption beyond the floodgate (the summoned fuel is effect-locked) — break the lock and the board is fairly inert. Evenly Matched is a near-clean answer. Early Ash/Imperm on the engine stops the plan before the lock exists.

matchups.howTheyWin: Fusion combo/control — Dracotail Faimena is a near-one-card starter (discard to Fusion Summon), chaining Dracotail fusions + a trap suite; often splashes the Branded engine for Mirrorjade.
matchups.gameplanFirst: Their disruption is bounce + negate, not destruction. Summon key monsters in DEFENSE to dodge Dracotail Horn (bounces Attack-position monsters), and don't let your win hinge on one bounce-able body.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Neutralise GULAMEL first (it pops a card every time you start a play). Then out Mirrorjade via Droplet / Kaiju / Evenly (NON-destruction). Dracotail Flame negates a Spell's effects — lead with monster/trap interaction, not Cyclone. BANISH their fusions (destroying them feeds the 2-cards-to-GY self-revive).

counterCards.name: Retaliating "C"
counterCards.notes: Pseudo-Dimension Shifter; their Spells trigger it

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Ash Branded Fusion (free Mirrorjade) or Ketu's search; Imperm Lukias (starves the set-trap fuel) or Arthalion's bounce. Faimena fuses on YOUR turn from hand — a board they 'pass' still interrupts you.
matchups.comboLine: Normal Lukias → add Faimena → discard Faimena (Quick — works on EITHER turn) to Fusion Summon; Lukias/Pan as material SET Dracotail traps (Pan also destroys) → Arthalion (bounces, self-revives) + Branded Fusion for Mirrorjade. Gulamel = a free pop on EVERY Dracotail activation.
matchups.weaknesses: Banishment beats it (engine self-revives from GY); Nibiru mid-loop; a precise early Imperm on Lukias starves it; Droll before the search chain. Open Nibiru + Droplet/Evenly going second is its nightmare. (This is the YCS Columbus 2026 WINNING deck — respect it.)

matchups.howTheyWin: 'Singer' fairies shuffle into the center zone to trigger + Synchro-climb. Elfnote Power Patron raises a center monster's Level +3 and immediately Synchro Summons — laddering into a wall of Synchro negates capped by Junora's WIDE field-negate.
matchups.gameplanFirst: Commit interruption as SET / face-down disruption — they end on multiple Synchro negates (omni-negates like Baronne / Crystal Wing). Force Power Patron out early, then handtrap it.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Clear the omni-negate (Baronne / Crystal Wing) first; use non-targeting / non-destruction, or just attack over the negate body once it's spent. Lava Golem / Nibiru side in; Evenly / DRNM / Droplet (the list itself fears these).

counterCards.name: Nibiru, the Primal Being
counterCards.notes: Heavy Special Summoning on the climb

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Imperm/Veiler/Ash Elfnote Power Patron or Medius the Pure (the Synchro/Fusion enablers) BEFORE the negate wall comes online. Droll after the first search. Ghost Belle on the GY recursion. (NOTE: this is the current Blazing Dominion build WITH 3× Regina.)
matchups.comboLine: Elfnote 'Singer' fairies (Lucina / Tinia / Regina / Fortuna) + the Power Patron engine (Elfnote Power Patron, Medius the Pure, Terminus portal) Synchro-climb through the Resonator / Red-Dragon-Archfiend package into RDA / Crimson King / Crystal Wing + Junoldo, recurring with Welcome Home / Rhapsodia traps.
matchups.weaknesses: Over-extends + searches a LOT — Maxx C / Droll / Fuwalos / Purulia wreck it (the list mains 3 Fuwalos + 2 Droll ITSELF). Bystials hit its DARK/LIGHT Synchro GY. Nibiru on the climb.

matchups.howTheyWin: Spellcaster engine fusing the Magistus equip package with Fairy Tail control. Fairy Tail - Wiccat 'double Foolish' fuels a search/recursion loop; ends on negates and grinds with Snow recursion + Magistus equips.
matchups.gameplanFirst: They can out-negate your handtraps (Teller/Zoroa negate; Zoroa also pops). Lead with a board they can't simply out-negate and pressure with Maxx C. They recur from GY (Snow + Wiccat sends) so Dimension Shifter is strong.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Zoroa of Daimon LOCKS your Extra Deck — prioritise MAIN-DECK / non-ED outs (Lightning Storm, Evenly, Raigeki-effects). Remove Daimon FIRST (it gates your whole strategy), bait the second Zoroa's negate+pop with a non-essential card, and clear the Field Spell before committing your ED.

counterCards.name: Infinite Impermanence
counterCards.notes: On the first Magistus/Fairy Tail effect

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Ash the Fairy Tail / Wiccat dump or the Artemis → Crowley add (the consistency hinges). Imperm Crowley or Rilliona (breaks the Xyz → Synchro Zoroa chain → no Daimon floodgate). HOLD your handtrap for the Magistus pivot, not the Fairy Tail opener. Droll the search loop.
matchups.comboLine: Fairy Tail (Lv4 Spellcasters + the unbanned Snow; Wiccat 'double Foolish' dumps Snow) feeds Magistus: equip Artemis → add Crowley → SS Crowley → Xyz Rilliona → SS Zoroa of Flame → Synchro into Zoroa of Daimon (Extra-Deck-effect floodgate), recurring Crowley/Rilliona to re-equip Magistus.
matchups.weaknesses: Combo-fragile — one Imperm/Ash at the Crowley/Rilliona pivot leaves only Fairy Tail bodies. Soft going first if it draws too many Fairy Tail bricks. Lightning Storm / Evenly / Cosmic cleanly answer the Zoroa board. Modest raw damage.

matchups.howTheyWin: Music-themed Synchro Tuner combo — the Kewl Tune monsters + Synchro Overtake / Kewl Tune Synchro climb the Synchro ladder fast and consistently, ending on a capping board (Super Starslayer TY-PHON locks Special Summons) plus Synchro disruption. A proactive going-first combo deck that also mains its own handtrap suite (Veiler / Ash / Belle / Fuwalos).
matchups.gameplanFirst: It's a combo deck — Maxx C / Mulcharmy / Nibiru pressure is strong. End on a board that survives a single Synchro negate; once TY-PHON locks Special Summons, prefer set / in-hand disruption over a board you'd need to rebuild.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Break the Synchro negates with NON-targeting / NON-destruction outs (Forbidden Droplet, Evenly Matched, Kaiju / Lava Golem). TY-PHON is a big body that locks Special Summons — out it WITHOUT relying on your own Special Summons. Nibiru punishes the multi-summon combo turn.

counterCards.name: Nibiru, the Primal Being
counterCards.notes: Heavy Special Summoning on the Synchro climb

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Ash / Imperm the search-and-Synchro hubs (Kewl Tune Cue / Reco / Synchro Overtake) BEFORE the climb resolves; Droll after their first add. They main Veiler / Ash / Belle / Fuwalos themselves, so one handtrap rarely ends them — pair it with pressure.
matchups.comboLine: Open a Kewl Tune Tuner + Synchro Overtake / Kewl Tune Synchro → Synchro-climb through the Kewl Tune Extra-Deck line (Track Maker / RS / Remix / B2B) into a capping board, ending on Super Starslayer TY-PHON (Special-Summon lock) plus Synchro disruption. Fydraulis Harmonia and JJ "Kewl Tune" extend / recover.
matchups.weaknesses: Combo-reliant — Maxx C / Droll / Nibiru and non-targeting going-second sweepers (Droplet / Evenly) all bite; it leans hard on its Synchro Extra Deck, so Extra-Deck hate + non-destruction removal get under the board. (Newer archetype — this is an initial intel pass; refine as results settle.)

matchups.howTheyWin: Going-first Fusion combo (post-Duelist Advance) — a one-card Gold Leo line builds a sticky Liger Dancer board, then double-attacks + grinds via GY recursion.
matchups.gameplanFirst: Hold interaction for the cards that lead to Liger Dancer (don't waste it early). Build a board that survives a Liger wipe — Liger only destroys SPECIAL-Summoned monsters, so a Normal-Summoned body or a continuous floodgate survives.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Liger Dancer is UNAFFECTED by your non-Lunalight effects — targeted removal/negation does nothing. Out it with a TRIBUTE: Kaiju / Lava Golem (removes it as a cost). Don't waste targeting into an open GY Crimson Fox (it negates + refunds LP). Watch S:P banishing one of yours face-down.

counterCards.name: Lava Golem
counterCards.notes: Tribute Liger away — ignores its immunity

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Ash Black Sheep or Kaleido Chick's deck-send (the consistency hubs). Imperm the fusion climb BEFORE Liger resolves — Liger is unaffected by non-Lunalight effects ONCE summoned. D.D. Crow/Belle on the GY loop / to pre-empt Crimson Fox.
matchups.comboLine: Black Sheep (discard → add a Lunalight S/T; banish from GY = Fusion substitute) + Polymerization → Perfume Dancer (search Tiger, recur) → Kaleido Chick sends material, Tiger revives → climb the Dance-Fusion ladder to Liger Dancer.
matchups.weaknesses: Far better going SECOND than first — turn-1 interaction outside Liger / S:P is thin. Needs Polymerization live (Solemn / Poly-hate stalls it). Crushed by attribute/type hate (Shadow-Imprisoning Mirror, Archnemeses Protos, Bystials). ROGUE / locals-strong.

matchups.howTheyWin: Cyberse Link-climbing combo that abuses BANISHMENT — Special Summons monsters back from banish to chain into Link bosses + a self-recurring loop, then grinds with the Maliss <C> disruption traps. Can OTK with Accesscode.
matchups.gameplanFirst: Build HARD negates / spot removal, NOT banish-pile hate (they recur from banish). Their traps need a face-up Maliss monster to fire — pre-emptively removing their first Maliss monster turns off the set traps. Dimension Shifter is strong (deck routes through GY/banish).
matchups.gameplanSecond: Hearts Crypter's banish CAN'T be negated while it points to a monster — disrupt on activation/cost or remove it before you commit. Clear the set <C> traps FIRST (remove their on-board Maliss to brick them). Lightning Storm / Evenly are strong; banish-pile hate is WEAK (they recur from banish).
matchups.chokepointTheirs: Droll is the single best card — drop after their FIRST add/search to collapse the loop. Ash the first <C> trap that SS/searches from Deck (TB-11 / GWC-06). Imperm White Rabbit ON SUMMON (deny the trap set) before a Link exists. They bait with a lesser Maliss — hold for the real set-from-Deck trap.
matchups.comboLine:
Maliss

White Rabbit (Normal/SS → SET a Maliss Trap from Deck) → activate that trap THE TURN it's set by banishing a face-up Maliss (which self-revives — free bodies): TB-11 SS from Deck, GWC-06 search + banish, MTP-07 revive → climb to the Queens (Hearts Crypter). The @Ignister package turns leftover bodies into Accesscode / extra Links.
matchups.weaknesses: Post-banlist consistency is shakier; DROLL completely walls it (it adds repeatedly); the turn-1 board is low-impact without @Ignister; folds to spot removal of its on-board Maliss (bricks the <C> traps).

matchups.howTheyWin: Mitsurugi grind core + a Rank-4 Ryzeal package (Ryzeal Detonator = free repeatable destroy). More going-second power and more layers than pure Mitsurugi.
matchups.gameplanFirst: Same as Mitsurugi — don't fold to one negate/wipe, keep discard fodder for Murakumo. Respect Ryzeal Detonator's free destroy on YOUR turn; bait its detach with a low-value card first.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Same Mitsurugi caveat — NON-destruction (tribute/banish) gets under the protected Ritual bodies. Detonator is a destroy effect — clear it FIRST or play around it (don't feed it). Mass non-targeting after baiting.

counterCards.name: Infinite Impermanence
counterCards.notes: Specifically strong on the Ice Ryzeal step

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Ash/Imperm ICE Ryzeal or King of the Feral Imps (the bridge into the Mitsurugi half) — denying it collapses the hybrid to half a deck. Droll after the first add. Nibiru on the summon-heavy line.
matchups.comboLine: Ryzeal starter → Rank-4 / King of the Feral Imps (the BRIDGE) → fetch the Mitsurugi enabler → run the ritual chain (Habakiri → Murakumo) AND bank Ryzeal Detonator (free repeatable destroy). Ends ~ Murakumo + Detonator + a Caesar/Photon negate + traps.
matchups.weaknesses: Combos through a NARROW bridge (King of the Feral Imps / the enabler) — handtrap that point and it folds. Droll brutal (two search engines). Extra-Deck-reliant (Droplet / ED hate bites).

matchups.howTheyWin: Reptile Ritual engine — one starter (Ame no Habakiri) becomes a wall of recurring Ritual monsters + searched traps, then grinds behind repeated negates and a board-wipe.
matchups.gameplanFirst: Build a board that doesn't fold to one negate or one wipe. Murakumo's negate is a soft 'discard-or-negate' — keep a SPARE card to feed the discard so your key effect resolves. End on disruption that doesn't need monsters on board (set traps) so Murakumo's re-summon wipe whiffs.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Saji/Aramasa make the board destruction-RESISTANT — Lightning Storm / Raigeki / DRNM are blanks. Break with NON-destruction: tribute (Kaiju / Underworld Goddess), banish, or bounce. Bait Murakumo with a throwaway, then push.
matchups.chokepointTheirs: Droll & Lock Bird is the single best card — after their FIRST search, Droll halts Prayers / Ritual / the second searcher. Ash the first searcher (Saji/Aramasa) or Prayers/Ritual. NOTE Murakumo's destroy is NOT Ash-able — spend Ash early.
matchups.comboLine: Searcher (Saji / Aramasa — self-tributing floaters) → add Ame no Habakiri + Mitsurugi Prayers / Ritual → Ritual-Summon up to Ame no Murakumo (on Special Summon: Raigeki-style destroy, once/turn). Tributing Mitsurugi monsters triggers their GY floats, so it reloads as it combos.
matchups.weaknesses: Search-dependent — Droll + double handtraps choke it; lower disruption density than top combo; Special-Summon / Ritual-lock floodgates gut it; non-destruction removal walks through its protection. (YCS Columbus 2026 Top 8.)

matchups.howTheyWin: Pendulum-flavored Fusion ladder — Medius the Pure adds/Special Summons Artmage Power Patron, Fusion-summons up the line, closes on Nerva (destruction-immune while a Field Spell is active) + the Nervedo Pendulum negate. Usually the Power Patron Artmage hybrid.
matchups.gameplanFirst: Their main interaction (Nervedo negate) triggers off THEIR plays, so on your turn you mostly fear held handtraps, not a board negate. Set up normally; just don't walk a key effect into a held negate, and be ready to remove the Field Spell next turn.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Nerva is destruction-immune ONLY while a card is active in the Field Spell Zone — clear/disable the Field Spell first (Cosmic Cyclone, Feather Duster), OR just use NON-destruction removal (banish/bounce/Kaiju/Lava Golem) to ignore the immunity. Remove Nerva, then the support.

counterCards.name: Cosmic Cyclone
counterCards.notes: Pop the Field Spell -> strips Nerva immunity

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Ash Blossom / Droll & Lock Bird on Medius the Pure (the search that assembles the Fusion line) — the two best counters. Imperm/Veiler on Medius or the first Power Patron. Disrupting the Field Spell strips Nerva's destruction immunity.
matchups.comboLine: Medius the Pure adds/SS Artmage Power Patron → Fusion-summon up the Power Patron ladder (Vidolium / Nervedo) → Nerva the Power Patron of Creation (destruction-immune while a card is active in the Field Spell Zone) + the Nervedo Pendulum negate; Artmage Fusion/traps add negation. (YCS Columbus 2026 Top 8.)
matchups.weaknesses: Reactive — its negate triggers off ITS plays, so on YOUR turn you mostly fear held handtraps. Ash / Droll on Medius collapses it. Clearing the Field Spell strips Nerva's immunity.
targetEndboard: Nerva the Power Patron of Creation

matchups.howTheyWin: Albaz/Branded fusion toolbox — spreads Predator Counters (turning your monsters into Lv1 fusion fodder), negates with Dragostapelia, OTKs with Triphyovertum. Mostly a Genesys/OCG/rogue pick in TCG.
matchups.gameplanFirst: Big danger is their Super Polymerization eating YOUR monsters on your turn (Predator Counters enable it) — avoid presenting two same-attribute monsters that complete a fusion. Their pure board is weak, so a normal board usually holds.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Predator Counters negate YOUR monsters' effects — lead with Spell/Trap or non-monster-effect removal (Lightning Storm / Evenly / Droplet). BANISH or tribute Triphyovertum (it self-revives from GY) — don't destroy. Then clear Dragostapelia / Wing Dragon.
matchups.chokepointTheirs: Ash Predaprimitive / Verte Anaconda (the Fusion-spell hubs); Imperm the first counter-engine fusion BEFORE counters land on your board. Nibiru on the climb to Triphyovertum.
matchups.comboLine: Predaprimitive (send a Predap to GY + tutor Polymerization; locks you to Plant/Dragon) or Verte Anaconda → spread Predator Counters (drop foes to Lv1) → Dragostapelia (negates effects of monsters carrying a counter) + Starving Venom Wing Dragon (sprays more counters) → climb to Predaplant Triphyovertum (negates Extra-Deck SS, gains ATK per counter, self-revives).
matchups.weaknesses: Reactive setup deck — one Ash/Imperm on Predaprimitive/Verte collapses the turn; slower than top combo; GY-banish hate stops Triphyovertum recursion. ROGUE in TCG (best evidence is an OCG regional Top 8).

matchups.howTheyWin: Quick-Play-Spell control/grind — its monsters trigger off activating quick-plays (Super Poly, Forbidden Droplet, Called by). Grinds with searchers + a monster-negate boss, recycles quick-plays, wins the long game. Often a Runick hybrid.
matchups.gameplanFirst: DON'T over-commit into Super Polymerization — diversify monster types/attributes to dodge it. Play around Varuroon's monster-negate + Mandate (MST omni-negate). Forcing a monster onto their field hurts the Sky Striker half (Quick-Plays want them with no monsters).
matchups.gameplanSecond: Clear set Spells / Multirole; Anti-Spell Fragrance + Denko Sekka shut their Spell plan down hard. Bait Varuroon's negate, then push.
matchups.chokepointTheirs: Imperm/Ash Radiant Typhoon Krosea (the double-searcher) AND the Sky Striker Engage — deny the searchers and the grind chokes. Droll the search loop.
matchups.comboLine: Radiant Typhoon + Sky Striker HYBRID (YCS Columbus Top 4) — quick-play Spells (Super Poly, Droplet, MST) trigger Radiant Typhoon monsters; the Sky Striker engine (Engage) draws + grinds. Ends on a low board with set Spells + a monster-negate boss; Mandate turns MST into an omni-negate.
matchups.weaknesses: Control/grind — loses to multi-negate boards and to Anti-Spell Fragrance (Spell-heavy). It folds if its searchers (Krosea / Engage) are denied.

matchups.howTheyWin: Spell-based control/grind — loops Sky Striker Mobilize - Engage! via the Ace monsters to out-resource you, disrupts with Widow Anchor / Shark Cannon. Wants its OWN monster zones EMPTY (most Quick-Plays need controlling no monsters).
matchups.gameplanFirst: Forcing a monster onto THEIR field genuinely matters — most Sky Striker Quick-Plays need them to control NO monsters, so a Kaiju / Lava Golem / token shuts off Widow Anchor + Afterburners on your turn. Set up normally; bait Widow Anchor with a non-essential monster.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Low board to 'break' — fight over resources + backrow. Clear MULTIROLE first (it shields + reloads their Spells), then the set Mecha Spells (Widow Anchor → Afterburners). Anti-Spell Fragrance / Imperial Order are the hardest counters.

counterCards.name: Lava Golem
counterCards.notes: Force a monster onto their field — shuts off Quick-Plays

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Droll & Lock Bird is the premier answer (chokes the Engage → Kagari add-loop); Ash the first Engage. Imperm is WEAK (their power is Spell-based, few monster targets). Cursed Seal of the Forbidden Spell permanently locks Engage.
matchups.comboLine: It's a SETUP turn, not a combo: dump 3+ Spells to GY (Engage etc.), keep your OWN monster zones EMPTY (Spells get bonuses when you control no monsters), end on Shizuku + 4–5 set Spells (Widow Anchor / Shark Cannon / Eagle Booster) + Multirole. Raye flips to Kagari (recur Engage) / Shizuku (search) reactively.
matchups.weaknesses: Grind deck with a LOW ceiling — loses to multi-negate boards it can't out one Spell at a time, and to Anti-Spell Fragrance / Imperial Order (its whole deck is Spells). Heavy backrow removal + going-second OTKs that ignore its low board hurt.

matchups.howTheyWin: Vanquish Soul K9 — a hand-resource 'tag-fighter' beatdown: reveal monsters in hand to fuel attribute effects, tag fighters in/out, grind resources. K9 adds bodies + punishes handtraps.
matchups.gameplanFirst: They're a grind/beatdown deck, not an OTK — a normal disruptive board is fine. Respect Caesar Valius's free destroy and its EARTH 'unaffected this turn' mode. Force them to commit before you tag interruption (they tag-out to dodge).
matchups.gameplanSecond: VS bodies are destruction-vulnerable UNLESS Caesar revealed EARTH (then unaffected that turn) — if so, attack over it / wait a turn. Evenly Matched is great (non-targeting). Answer the K9 reactive Xyz FIRST (it's the on-your-turn negate), then the VS boss.

counterCards.name: Infinite Impermanence
counterCards.notes: Trap — slips past K9 Ripper's negate

matchups.chokepointTheirs: ⚠ The K9 half KEYS OFF your monster-effect activations from hand/GY — firing a MONSTER handtrap (Ash / Veiler) can arm K9-17 Ripper + ambush-summon Izuna/Lupus. PREFER Infinite Impermanence (a Trap). Ash Razen / Stake / Start (the search shell) deliberately.
matchups.comboLine: VS monsters (all EARTH / FIRE / DARK) reveal monsters in hand for attribute effects; Razen searches; Stake your Soul! / VS Start! tutor. Heavy Borger / Caesar Valius SS themselves by returning a VS of a different Type. The K9 half bolts on a reactive Rank-5 Xyz toolbox (Ripper / Izuna / Lupus) that punishes the opponent's monster-effect activations.
matchups.weaknesses: Grind/control with a modest turn-1 board — combo decks that go UNDER it deny K9 its triggers; it must HOLD reveal fodder; Spell/Trap interaction (Imperm / Evenly / Droplet) avoids feeding K9; Dimension Shifter hurts K9's GY summons. Strong-rogue post-banlist, not Tier-1.

matchups.howTheyWin: Synchro Spellcasters/Illusions whose Special Summons + Sinful Spoils cards trigger White Forest spells to grind, then lock you out with a stacked multi-disruption board. Azamina splash adds a recyclable negate (Ilia Silvia).
matchups.gameplanFirst: Bait Ilia Silvia's omni-negate (it costs a tribute) on a non-essential effect, then resolve your payoff; expect a non-targeting banish (Chaos Angel / S:P) so don't rely on one fragile piece.
matchups.gameplanSecond: Clear the omni-negate first. NON-targeting / NON-destruction removal (Kaiju, Lava Golem, send/banish, Evenly) — avoid pure destruction (their Synchros gain protection). Watch Silvera's Book of Eclipse flipping your board face-down.

counterCards.name: Effect Veiler
counterCards.notes: Cleaner than Imperm — they fuel off Imperm

matchups.chokepointTheirs: Imperm/Ash the early starter (Elzette / Silvy) before it converts into multiple bodies; Droll the search loop; Ash the Ilia Silvia / Hallowed Azamina recycle. Effect VEILER is cleaner than Imperm (they fuel off Imperm).
matchups.comboLine: White Forest spells (Silvy / Elzette / Rciela) trigger off Synchro Summons + Sinful Spoils to grind cards, Synchro-climbing (Diabellstar / Azamina) into negates + non-targeting banish; the Azamina splash adds Ilia Silvia (a recyclable omni-negate).
matchups.weaknesses: Turns your DEAD handtraps into fuel, so ONE rarely ends them — but Droll chokes the search loop, non-targeting/non-destruction is needed, and Bystials hit their GY.
targetEndboard: Sinful Spoils of the White Forest

matchups.howTheyWin: LIGHT-Beast Synchro combo (Cupsy/Cooky/Lollipo + Yummy Way Synchros) that swarms cheaply and, in the Azamina/Fiendsmith build, converts into multiple Fusion omni-negates.
matchups.gameplanFirst: It's a combo deck — Maxx C / Mulcharmy pressure is strong. The Azamina layer makes them hard to handtrap outside the Standby Phase, so end on disruption that doesn't rely on perfect timing. Dimension Shifter shuts the Fiendsmith GY engine off hard.
matchups.gameplanSecond: No-negate sweepers shine — Dark Ruler No More / Forbidden Droplet turn off the Fusion negates. Bystials banish their LIGHT Fiendsmith GY. Lead NON-targeting (dodges S:P's banish + Desirae protection), then clear S:P, and watch Lacrima's quick summon on your turn.
matchups.chokepointTheirs: Ash Fiendsmith's Tract (the search + GY-Fusion enabler) — highest value. Imperm Engraver (the equip hub). Yummy Snatchy's Field-Spell placement CANNOT be Ash'd. Belle / Mulcharmy Purulia punish the GY-Fusion engine.
matchups.comboLine: Yummy core (Marshmao SS's itself if you control no / only-LIGHT-Beast; Cooky/Cupsy swarm + dig) → pivot to Fiendsmith: Tract (search a LIGHT Fiend + discard; GY-banish to Fusion) → Requiem → tribute to SS Engraver from Deck, equip → Necroquip Princess → Sequence shuffles Lacrima + 2 Engraver for Desirae (negate). Azamina adds Ilia Silvia.
matchups.weaknesses: GY-DEPENDENT — Dimension Shifter / macro is brutal; handtrap-vulnerable at the Fiendsmith pivot (Ash on Tract / Imperm on Engraver stops the disruption suite); Droll mid-combo; Nibiru on the multi-summon turn.

formats.notes: Auto-built from ygoprodeck tournament lists + researched strategy (see docs/META_2026-05.md). Pick your deck as primary, then refine each matchup. Each opponent's typical end board is pre-filled — open Testing -> Going second to practise breaking it.
